title: Simpler
summary: Load and play any audio sample from MIDI. The engine inside every Drum Rack pad — also works standalone for melodic samples, one-shots, or chopped loops.
steps: Drag 'Simpler' from Instruments in the browser onto a new MIDI track — or find it inside a Drum Rack pad by clicking the pad and pressing the chain icon
Drag an audio file (wav/aiff/mp3) from the browser directly onto the waveform display to load it
Classic mode: sample plays with pitch tracking from MIDI notes (use for melodic samples, pads, bass notes)
1-Shot mode: sample plays once from start on note-on, ignores MIDI note-off (use for drums — the sample finishes naturally)
Slicing mode: Simpler automatically slices a loop at transients and maps each slice to a MIDI pad (use for drum loops you want to rearrange or chop)
Adjust sample start and end points by dragging the markers on the waveform — useful for removing silence or isolating a specific hit
In the Amp section: Attack controls how fast the sample fades in. Decay/Sustain/Release shape the tail. For a drum hit: Attack 0ms, Decay to taste, Sustain 0 (so it dies after decay)
tips: In Classic mode, enable 'Loop' to make the sample loop while a MIDI note is held — great for sustained pads from a short sample
Warp is off by default in Simpler — if your loop drifts in tempo, enable Warp and set it to the loop's original BPM
In Slicing mode, right-click the sliced MIDI clip and 'Show in New MIDI Track' — each slice is a separate note you can rearrange freely
The internal LFO and Filter inside Simpler can modulate pitch, volume, or filter cutoff without needing extra devices
ableton_version_note: Available in all editions of Live 9+. Slicing mode requires Live Standard or Suite.
